description:
You are helping a QA engineer prepare a reproducible Python test environment for an upcoming regression run.  
Follow these exact requirements:

1. Create a new virtual environment at **/home/user/qa_env**.  
2. Activate that environment and install **exactly** the following two packages and versions (no extras, no newer versions, no additional dependency constraints):  
   • requests==2.28.2  
   • pytest==7.4.0  
3. Immediately after the installation, produce a plain-text log file named **/home/user/qa_env_setup.log** that contains *only* the two installed packages in **alphabetical order**, each on its own line, with no leading/trailing spaces or extra blank lines.

Required log content – *exactly*:

pytest==7.4.0  
requests==2.28.2  

4. Nothing else should be written to the log (no headers, timestamps, or comments).

Automated tests will verify:
• The directory /home/user/qa_env exists and is a valid virtual environment (bin/activate must be present).  
• Within that environment, the versions of requests and pytest match the ones specified.  
• The file /home/user/qa_env_setup.log exists and its contents are **exactly** as shown above (including newline placement).  
No root privileges are available, so everything must be done inside /home/user.  
A typical solution can be achieved with roughly 3–4 terminal commands; use any approach that meets the requirements.
truth:
Expected end-state reference for automated grading:

1. Directory /home/user/qa_env/ must exist and contain:
   /home/user/qa_env/bin/activate (regular file, readable)
   /home/user/qa_env/bin/pip (executable)
   /home/user/qa_env/lib/python*/site-packages/pytest/__init__.py
   /home/user/qa_env/lib/python*/site-packages/requests/__init__.py

2. Inside the virtual environment’s site-packages, pip show must report:
   Name: requests
   Version: 2.28.2

   Name: pytest
   Version: 7.4.0

3. The log file /home/user/qa_env_setup.log must exist with exactly 2 lines, ASCII text, newline-terminated at the end of the last line:

Line 1: “pytest==7.4.0\n”  
Line 2: “requests==2.28.2\n”

(There must be no extra trailing newline after line 2.)

4. File permissions: default user-writable (0600–0644 acceptable).
